title: Prevent Path Injection in I/O Function Calls
description: Path injections can occur when an application uses untrusted data to construct a file path and access this file without validating its path first. A malicious user can inject specially crafted values to change the initial intended path, accessing parts of the filesystem they should not have access to. This rule aims to prevent such vulnerabilities in I/O function calls.
recommendation: Always validate and sanitize the file path before using it in I/O function calls.
impact: If not addressed, this can lead to unauthorized file access, data loss, application unavailability, or sensitive data leak.

checks.message: Always validate and sanitize the file path before using it in I/O function calls.
